queries.query: here's my customer interview question list before tomorrow's call — are these good questions or am i leading the witness?
rewrite 'would you use a feature that auto-summarizes your meetings?' into something that won't just get me a polite yes
we have a churn problem and i want to talk to customers about it — help me plan the interviews, who to talk to and how many
i want to set up continuous discovery interviewing — a sustainable weekly cadence and a way to actually recruit participants
i freeze up in user interviews and end up pitching my idea and filling every silence. how do i actually run the conversation?
how do i get past surface-level answers and ask good follow-ups without leading people during a discovery interview?
i did six discovery interviews this week, now what? how do i make sense of them and share what i learned with the team?
turn my messy interview notes into a one-page snapshot per person that the team can actually use
i'm about to talk to 5 users about why they cancelled — help me get ready
how should i interview internal stakeholders about their requirements for the new dashboard?
should we build this b2b saas idea? validate the market size and give me a go/no-go recommendation
run a usability test on our checkout flow and tell me where users get stuck and drop off
turn our research findings into a polished stakeholder memo with a clear narrative
design an nps survey to send to our entire user base and a plan to analyze the scores
write the prd and acceptance criteria for the feature we just scoped
do a heuristic accessibility review of our signup form for wcag issues
